result_legend_force: F - traction force, N. Shows the force needed to pull one tool through soil.
result_legend_resistance: q - specific resistance, N/m. Force divided by tool width; lower is better for comparison.
result_legend_power: P - drawbar power, kW. Mechanical power required at the selected speed.
result_legend_fuel: Q - fuel consumption, l/h. Estimated diesel consumption for one tool.

instruction.title: Instructions
instruction.intro: Read these instructions before using the application. They describe the purpose of the program, calculation meaning, field limits, buttons, menus, and windows.
instruction.section.overview.title: Application Purpose
instruction.section.overview.body:
The application calculates energy parameters for soil tiller tools: traction force, specific resistance, drawbar power, and estimated fuel consumption. It helps compare the built-in KPS-4.0 tool, the experimental tool, and custom tools from the tool manager.
The main window contains calculation parameters, graphs, and results. Change the parameters, choose the modes, and press Calculate.
instruction.section.calculations.title: How Calculations Work
instruction.section.calculations.body:
F is traction force in newtons. For the selected speed, force is found from the tool reference points by linear interpolation and then scaled proportionally to the selected depth: deeper tillage means higher force.
q is specific resistance in N/m. It equals F divided by tool width. Lower q is better for comparisons.
P is drawbar power in kW. Speed is converted from km/h to m/s, then power is calculated as F * v / 1000.
Q is fuel consumption in l/h. It is an estimate based on power, specific fuel consumption, and fuel density from the tool profile.
In comparison mode the application also shows the better tool and percentage difference. In auto-optimization mode it searches speeds from minimum to maximum with the configured step and chooses the speed with the lowest average q.
instruction.section.depth.title: Depth
instruction.section.depth.body:
The Depth field sets working depth H in centimeters. The default allowed range is 5 to 20 cm. If the value is outside the range or is not a number, the application shows a warning and uses 10 cm.
The limits can be changed with Custom depth limits. When enabled, minimum and maximum depth fields appear. Minimum must be less than maximum; otherwise the application returns to 5-20 cm.
instruction.section.speed.title: Speed
instruction.section.speed.body:
The Speed field is used in manual speed mode. The default allowed range is 5 to 12 km/h. If the value is invalid, the application shows a warning and uses 8 km/h.
In automatic mode the speed field is disabled: the application selects the optimal speed within the active range.
instruction.section.tool_mode.title: Tool Mode
instruction.section.tool_mode.body:
Single tool calculates values only for the selected first tool.
Compare KPS and experimental always uses the two built-in assignment tools: KPS-4.0 and the experimental tool. Tool selectors are locked in this mode.
Compare selected tools lets you choose the first and second tool manually. Built-in and custom tools are available.
instruction.section.speed_mode.title: Speed Mode
instruction.section.speed_mode.body:
Manual speed uses the value from the speed field.
Find optimal speed searches from the active minimum to the active maximum speed. The search step is configured through Settings -> Optimization step. The step must be positive; invalid input falls back to 0.5 km/h.
instruction.section.limits.title: Custom Limits
instruction.section.limits.body:
Custom speed limits and Custom depth limits in the settings menu enable extra boundary fields. These limits make the calculation differ from the original assignment, so the application shows a warning when they are enabled.
The minimum value must always be lower than the maximum value. Invalid limits disable the custom mode and restore the defaults: speed 5-12 km/h and depth 5-20 cm.
instruction.section.results.title: Results
instruction.section.results.body:
The results panel shows the current depth, speed, symbol legend, and calculation lines for each selected tool. Each tool line contains F, q, P, and Q.
If two tools are selected, a Better line appears with the tool that has lower q and the percentage difference. Auto-optimization also shows the selected optimal speed and minimum average q.
instruction.section.graphs.title: Graphs
instruction.section.graphs.body:
The graph area plots traction force F and specific resistance q versus speed at the current depth. Even if a single custom tool is selected, the graphs keep the built-in KPS-4.0 and experimental tools as required reference lines.
The speed axis follows the active speed limits. The graphs are redrawn automatically when the window is resized.
instruction.section.file_menu.title: File Menu
instruction.section.file_menu.body:
Import JSON loads settings and custom tools from a file. Tools with the same ID are replaced by imported ones.
Export JSON saves the current configuration to a file.
Change config location moves the main config.json to the selected path. Use default config location returns storage to the user application folder.
Instructions opens this window without strict mode. Changelog shows bundled release notes. About opens version, update, and license details. Exit closes the application.
instruction.section.tools_menu.title: Tools Menu
instruction.section.tools_menu.body:
The Tools item opens the custom tool manager. There you can add a new profile, duplicate an existing one, delete a custom profile, and save changes.
Built-in tools cannot be deleted or overwritten: duplicate them first, then edit the copy.
instruction.section.settings_menu.title: Settings Menu
instruction.section.settings_menu.body:
The settings menu switches the interface language, enables custom speed and depth limits, enables the beautiful interface, shows or hides help icons, and configures the optimization step.
All these preferences are saved to the JSON config and restored on the next launch.
instruction.section.tool_manager.title: Tool Manager
instruction.section.tool_manager.body:
The list on the left shows all available tools. An asterisk marks a built-in tool.
ID is the unique profile identifier. Name appears in results and graph legends. Width in meters is used for q. Base depth is used to scale force to the actual depth. Color and line style control graph rendering.
Reference points are entered one per line as speed:force, for example 6:400. At least two points with different speeds and positive force are required.
instruction.section.secondary_windows.bo:
Changelog shows bundled release records and does not require internet access.
About shows the version, launch method, current timestamp, author, license, and update status. Update checking contacts GitHub and shows an error message if it fails.
The license window contains the MIT license text. These windows can be closed without affecting calculations.
matplotlib_missing: matplotlib is not installed. Install project dependencies to show graphs.
